name: Chayotes with Corn and Chiles
recipeIngredient: 2 fresh Anaheim or poblano chiles, charred, peeled, and diced
1 cup frozen or fresh corn kernels (from 2 ears of corn)
1/2 tsp red chile powder, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart saucepan, sauté the chopped onion in olive oil until translucent.
2. Peel the chayotes (they have thin skin and can be easily peeled with a swivel-blade peeler), then cut each into halves, scoop out and discard the seeds, and dice the flesh. Add to the saucepan along with the diced chiles, corn, salt, pepper, and evaporated milk.
3. Cover and gently simmer for about 15 minutes, or until the chayotes are tender.
4. Stir in the grated Parmesan cheese and cook for a few seconds until the sauce thickens.
5. Transfer to a serving bowl and sprinkle with grated sharp cheddar cheese and red chile powder.
6. Serve as a vegetarian entree or alongside grilled meats.
